content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 31, 1987


Earl Wilson, City Manager
City of Gustine
P. O. Box 306
Gustine, CA 95322

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-070

Dear Mr. Wilson:

This is in response to your request for advice with regard to the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

When a city contracts with a law firm to provide services as city attorney, is the firm required to file a statement of economic interests, or is the individual attorney required to file the statement?

CONCLUSION

The individual attorney who provides services to the city must file the statement of economic interests.

ANALYSIS

In Section 81002, the Act states as one of its purposes that:

Assets and income of public officials which may be materially affected by their official actions should be disclosed and in appropriate circumstances


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Earl Wilson, City Manager
March 31, 1987
Page 2


       the officials should be disqualified from acting in
order that conflicts of interest may be avoided.

                                          Section 81002(c).

       The Act defines "public official" as "every member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government
agency" (Section 82048), and specifies the public officials,
including city attorneys, who are required to file statements
of economic interests (Form 721).  (Sections 87200-87208.)

       Based on the foregoing, it is clear that the conflict of
interest disclosure provisions of the Act apply to individuals
who are employed by or provide services to state and local govern-
ment agencies, and not to business entities which contract to
provide services to such agencies.  Therefore, when a law firm
is hired to provide services as city attorney, the individual
attorney who provides the services must file periodic statements
of economic interests (Form 721) pursuant to Section 87200.

       If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate
to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          Diane M. Griffiths
                                          General Counsel

                                          [signature]
                                   By    Carla Wardlow
                                          Political Reform Consultant

City of Justine
682 Third Avenue
P.O. Box 306
Justine, California 95322

Earl Wilson
Sassed X House
City Manager - City Clerk

FPPC
MAR 2 10 02 AM '87

Phone
(209) 854-6471

February 27, 1987


Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca.  95804-0807

This is in regard to the amendment to the Political Reform Act
(1985 Stats. Chapter 611) which requires City Attorneys to file a
Form 721. If the City Attorney services are contracted rather
than being performed by an employee, is the contract individual
or firm required to make a filing?

Your response as soon as possible is appreciated. If you need
any further details regarding our City Attorney services to answer
this request, please contact me.

Very Truly Yours,

Earl Wilson
City Manager

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 3, 1987

Earl Wilson
City Manager
City of Gustine
P.O. Box 306
Irvine, CA 95322

Re: 87-070

Dear Mr. Wilson:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on March 2, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
